title: Child Development Account
text:
Child Development Account - SupportGoWhere
Child Development Account
Share link
Special savings account for the child, which can be used for approved areas of child-raising expenditure, including healthcare and preschool fees.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
CDA First Step Grant enhanced by $2,000 for eligible Singaporean children
Up to $15,000 in Government co-matching for eligible Singaporean children
Enhancements will apply to eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 February 2023
Collapse all sections
About this support

The Child Development Account (CDA) is a special savings account for the child, which can be used for defray child-raising costs. The First Step Grant is automatically deposited after the CDA is opened, and subsequent savings will be co-matched by the Government, up to a cap.

Eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 Feb 2023 will qualify for the following enhanced CDA benefits announced at Budget 2023.

Child Development Account First Step Grant:

$5,000 automatically credited into the CDA upon account opening.

Government co-matching of CDA savings:

Eligible Singaporean children are entitled to Government co-matching up to a cap of $4,000 for the first child, $7,000 for the second child, $9,000 for the third and fourth child, and $15,000 for the fifth child and beyond.

Who is this for?

Child is a Singapore Citizen

Children born on or after 14 Feb 2023

Parents are lawfully married

Children of unwed parents may be eligible for CDA benefits if they are born on or after 1 September 2016.

How to apply?

All parents can apply for Baby Bonus Scheme at LifeSG:

Bundled service together with child’s birth registration

Direct application for child who has already registered for birth

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSF Consolidated Hotline: 1800 111 2222 

(say ‘Baby Bonus’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)




Operating hours: Mon to Fri 8.30am to 6pm | Saturday: 8.30am to 1pm

(Closed on Public Holidays)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines




Submit an enquiry through Ask Baby Bonus




Scheme last updated 27 Nov 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
